annotations.label: Group action

anchor.snippet: is, loosely speaking, an operation that takes an element of

annotations.note: `MulAction α β` is exactly a monoid/group action with `one_smul` and `mul_smul`.

annotations.label: Representation of a group

anchor.snippet: is called a representation of the group

annotations.note: `Representation k G V` is the homomorphism `G →* (V →ₗ[k] V)`, i.e. a linear group representation.

annotations.label: Symmetric group acts by permutation

anchor.snippet: by permuting the elements of the set

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm.applyMulAction` is the action of the permutation group `Equiv.Perm α` on `α`.

annotations.label: Left group action

anchor.snippet: is a group with identity element

annotations.note: Mathlib's `MulAction` is the standard left action satisfying `one_smul` and `mul_smul`.

annotations.label: Action as homomorphism into symmetric group

anchor.snippet: one may equivalently define a group action of

annotations.note: `MulAction.toPermHom : G →* Equiv.Perm α` realizes the action as a homomorphism, but the equivalence with actions is not bundled as a single iff.

annotations.label: Right group action

anchor.snippet: Likewise, a right group action of

annotations.note: Mathlib has no dedicated right-action class; right actions are modeled as left `MulAction`s of the opposite monoid `Gᵐᵒᵖ`.

annotations.label: Relation between left and right actions

anchor.snippet: The difference between left and right actions is in the order in which a product gh acts on x

annotations.note: The left/right correspondence is captured only implicitly through the opposite-monoid action `MulOpposite.instMulAction`, not as a stated equivalence.

annotations.label: Faithful (effective) action

anchor.snippet: The action is called faithful or effective if

annotations.note: `FaithfulSMul M α` says distinct scalars act differently, i.e. the action is faithful.

annotations.label: Free action

anchor.snippet: The action is called free (or semiregular or fixed-point free )

annotations.note: Freeness has no dedicated predicate; it is expressed pointwise as `stabilizer α b = ⊥` (as used in `selfEquivOrbitsQuotientProd`).

annotations.label: Group acting on itself is free (Cayley)

anchor.snippet: the action of any group on itself by left multiplication is free

annotations.note: The left-regular action `Monoid.toMulAction` is provably faithful on cancellative monoids, but its freeness for groups is not stated as a named lemma (nor is Cayley's theorem).

annotations.label: Transitive action

anchor.snippet: is called transitive if for any two points

annotations.note: `MulAction.IsPretransitive` asserts that for any `x y` there is `g` with `g • x = y`.

annotations.label: Simply transitive (regular) action

anchor.snippet: The action is simply transitive (or sharply transitive , or regular )

annotations.note: `AddTorsor` is exactly a simply transitive additive group action; no general multiplicative `IsSimplyTransitive` predicate exists.

annotations.label: n-transitive action

anchor.snippet: with pairwise distinct entries

annotations.note: `IsMultiplyPretransitive G α n` is pretransitivity on `Fin n ↪ α`, i.e. transitivity on n-tuples of distinct points.

annotations.label: Sharply n-transitive action

anchor.snippet: the action on tuples without repeated entries in

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of sharp/regular n-transitivity (simple transitivity on injective n-tuples).

annotations.label: Symmetric/alternating group transitivity

anchor.snippet: The action of the symmetric group of X is transitive

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm.isMultiplyPretransitive` proves `Sym(α)` is n-transitive for all n, with `alternatingGroup.isMultiplyPretransitive` for the alternating group.

annotations.label: General linear group action on nonzero vectors

anchor.snippet: The action of the general linear group of a vector space V

annotations.note: The GL action on `V` exists via `LinearEquiv.applyDistribMulAction`, but transitivity on nonzero vectors is not a stated result.

annotations.label: Primitive action

anchor.snippet: is called primitive if there is no partition of X

annotations.note: `IsPreprimitive` is transitivity together with every block being trivial, the standard definition of primitivity.

annotations.label: Wandering action

anchor.snippet: The action is wandering if every

annotations.note: No notion of a wandering point/action is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Point of discontinuity

anchor.snippet: is called a point of discontinuity for the action of

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define points of discontinuity for a group action.

annotations.label: Properly discontinuous action

anchor.snippet: The action is properly discontinuous if for every compact subset

annotations.note: `ProperlyDiscontinuousSMul` requires finitely many group elements moving any compact set to meet another, the standard definition.

annotations.label: Freely discontinuous action

anchor.snippet: Actions with this property are sometimes called freely discontinuous

annotations.note: The 'freely discontinuous' strengthening is not formalized as a named concept.

annotations.label: Cocompact action

anchor.snippet: is called cocompact if there exists a compact subset

annotations.note: There is no notion of a cocompact group action (the `Filter.cocompact` is unrelated).

annotations.label: Continuous action

anchor.snippet: The action is said to be continuous if the map

annotations.note: `ContinuousSMul M X` requires joint continuity of `(g,x) ↦ g • x`.

annotations.label: Proper action

anchor.snippet: The action is said to be proper if the map

annotations.note: `ProperSMul` requires `(g,x) ↦ (g • x, x)` to be a proper map, the standard definition of a proper action.

annotations.label: Locally free action

anchor.snippet: It is said to be locally free if there exists a neighbourhood

annotations.note: Local freeness of an action is not formalized.

annotations.label: Strongly continuous action

anchor.snippet: The action is said to be strongly continuous if the orbital map

annotations.note: Strong continuity (continuity of each orbital map) is not formalized as a named concept for actions.

annotations.label: Smooth points / Lie group action

anchor.snippet: then the subspace of smooth points for the action

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define the subspace of smooth points of a Lie group action.

annotations.label: Irreducible action

anchor.snippet: the action is said to be irreducible if there are no proper nonzero

annotations.note: Irreducibility of a linear action is the simplicity (`IsSimpleModule`) of the corresponding module, but there is no dedicated `Representation`-level irreducibility predicate.

annotations.label: Semisimple action

anchor.snippet: It is said to be semisimple if it decomposes as a direct sum

annotations.note: `IsSemisimpleRepresentation` (equiv. `IsSemisimpleModule` over `k[G]`) is the semisimplicity of a linear action.

annotations.label: Orbit

anchor.snippet: The orbit of an element x in X is the set of elements

annotations.note: `MulAction.orbit M a = Set.range (· • a)` is exactly the orbit.

anchors.snippet: The orbit of an element x in X is the set of elements

annotations.label: Orbits partition the set

anchor.snippet: form a partition of X

annotations.note: `orbitRel` is the `Setoid` 'same orbit', so its equivalence classes (the orbits) partition the set.

annotations.label: Transitive iff one orbit

anchor.snippet: The group action is transitive if and only if it has exactly one orbit

annotations.note: `pretransitive_iff_subsingleton_quotient` says transitivity is equivalent to the orbit quotient being a subsingleton (one orbit).

annotations.label: Quotient of the action

anchor.snippet: is called the quotient of the action

annotations.note: `orbitRel.Quotient G α` is the quotient of `α` by the orbit equivalence relation.

annotations.label: Invariant subset

anchor.snippet: The subset Y is said to be invariant under G if

annotations.note: `IsInvariantBlock G B := ∀ g, g • B ⊆ B` is exactly G-invariance of a subset.

annotations.label: Fixed subset

anchor.snippet: The subset Y is called fixed under G if

annotations.note: A pointwise-fixed subset is `Y ⊆ fixedPoints G X`; there is no dedicated subset predicate (Mathlib's `IsFixedBlock` is setwise, hence different).

annotations.label: Orbits are invariant subsets

anchor.snippet: Every orbit is an invariant subset of X on which G acts transitively

annotations.note: `IsFixedBlock.orbit` shows each orbit is invariant, and the orbit carries a pretransitive sub-action instance.

annotations.label: G-invariant element

anchor.snippet: A G -invariant element of X is

annotations.note: `fixedPoints M α = {a | ∀ m, m • a = a}` is exactly the set of G-invariant elements.

annotations.label: Fixed point of an element

anchor.snippet: x is a fixed point of g

annotations.note: `fixedBy α m = {x | m • x = x}` is the set of fixed points of a single element `m`.

anchors.snippet: x is a fixed point of g

annotations.label: Stabilizer subgroup

anchor.snippet: the stabilizer subgroup of G with respect to x

annotations.note: `MulAction.stabilizer G a` is the subgroup `{g | g • a = a}`.

anchors.snippet: the stabilizer subgroup of G with respect to x

annotations.label: Stabilizers along an orbit are conjugate

anchor.snippet: the two stabilizer groups G x and G y are related by

annotations.note: `stabilizer_smul_eq_stabilizer_map_conj` gives `stabilizer G (g • a) = (stabilizer G a).map (conj g)`.

annotations.label: Orbit type / principal orbit type

anchor.snippet: the stabilizers of elements in the same orbit are conjugate to each other

annotations.note: Conjugacy of stabilizers within an orbit is `stabilizerEquivStabilizerOfOrbitRel`, but the (principal) orbit-type terminology itself is not formalized.

annotations.label: Orbit–stabilizer theorem

anchor.snippet: This result is known as the orbit–stabilizer theorem

annotations.note: `orbitEquivQuotientStabilizer` gives `orbit α b ≃ α ⧸ stabilizer α b`, with the cardinality version `card_orbit_mul_card_stabilizer_eq_card_group`.

anchors.snippet: This result is known as the orbit–stabilizer theorem

anchors.value: {\displaystyle f(g)=f(h)\iff g{\cdot }x=h{\cdot }x\iff g^{-1}h{\cdot }x=x\iff g^{-1}h\in G_{x}\iff h\in gG_{x}.}

annotations.label: Orbit length divides group order

anchor.snippet: If G is finite then the orbit–stabilizer theorem, together with Lagrange's theorem

annotations.note: Divisibility follows immediately from the orbit-stabilizer product `card_orbit_mul_card_stabilizer_eq_card_group`, but is not stated as a standalone dvd lemma.

anchors.snippet: If G is finite then the orbit–stabilizer theorem, together with Lagrange's theorem

annotations.label: Prime-order action fixed-point count

anchor.snippet: Let G be a group of prime order p acting on a set X with k elements

annotations.note: `IsPGroup.card_modEq_card_fixedPoints` gives `card α ≡ card (fixedPoints) [MOD p]` for any p-group, which includes the prime-order case.

annotations.label: Counting cube graph automorphisms

anchor.snippet: We can use the orbit–stabilizer theorem to count the automorphisms of a graph

annotations.note: This specific worked example of counting cube-graph automorphisms is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Burnside's lemma

anchor.snippet: A result closely related to the orbit–stabilizer theorem is Burnside's lemma

annotations.note: `sum_card_fixedBy_eq_card_orbits_mul_card_group` is exactly Burnside's lemma (sum of fixed points = #orbits × |G|).

anchors.snippet: A result closely related to the orbit–stabilizer theorem is Burnside's lemma

annotations.label: Burnside ring

anchor.snippet: forms a ring called the Burnside ring of G

annotations.note: The Burnside ring of a group is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trivial action

anchor.snippet: The trivial action of any group G on any set X is defined by

annotations.note: There is no named trivial-action instance (it would create instance diamonds), though it is trivially expressible.

annotations.label: Left multiplication on itself

anchor.snippet: left multiplication is an action of G on G

annotations.note: `Monoid.toMulAction : MulAction M M` is the left-multiplication (regular) action.

annotations.label: Left multiplication on cosets

anchor.snippet: left multiplication is an action of G on the set of cosets

annotations.note: `MulAction.quotient` gives the left action `MulAction β (α ⧸ H)` on cosets.

annotations.label: Conjugation action

anchor.snippet: conjugation is an action of G on G

annotations.note: `ConjAct G` is the type alias whose `MulAction` on `G` is conjugation.

annotations.label: Conjugation on conjugates of a subgroup

anchor.snippet: conjugation is an action of G on conjugates of H

annotations.note: `ConjAct G` acts by conjugation on subgroups (via the pointwise action), whose orbit of `H` is its set of conjugates, though this orbit description is not packaged separately.

annotations.label: Action of Z as an automorphism

anchor.snippet: An action of Z on a set X uniquely determines and is determined by an automorphism

annotations.note: The bijection between ℤ-actions and a single automorphism is not formalized as an equivalence.

annotations.label: Symmetric group on {1,...,n}

anchor.snippet: The symmetric group S n and its subgroups act on the set

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm.applyMulAction` with `α = Fin n` gives `S_n` acting on `{1,…,n}`.

annotations.label: Symmetry group of a polyhedron

anchor.snippet: The symmetry group of a polyhedron acts on the set of vertices

annotations.note: This specific geometric example (polyhedron symmetry group on vertices) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Symmetry group of a geometric object

anchor.snippet: The symmetry group of any geometrical object acts on the set of points

annotations.note: No generic 'symmetry group acts on its geometric object' construction is formalized.

annotations.label: Scalar multiplication

anchor.snippet: For a coordinate space V over a field F with group of units

annotations.note: `Units.instMulAction` makes the group of units `Fˣ` act on a module by scalar multiplication.

annotations.label: Automorphism group action

anchor.snippet: The automorphism group of a vector space

annotations.note: `LinearEquiv.applyDistribMulAction` is the faithful action of `M ≃ₗ[R] M` (the automorphism group) on the module `M`.

annotations.label: General linear group on K^n

anchor.snippet: The general linear group GL( n , K ) and its subgroups, particularly its Lie subgroups

annotations.note: GL acting on `Kⁿ` is the special case `M = Fin n → K` of `LinearEquiv.applyDistribMulAction`.

annotations.label: GL(n,Z) acting on Z^n

anchor.snippet: acts on Z n by natural matrix action

annotations.note: GL(n,ℤ) on ℤⁿ is the special case `M = Fin n → ℤ` of `LinearEquiv.applyDistribMulAction`.

annotations.label: Affine group on affine space

anchor.snippet: The affine group acts transitively on the points of an affine space

annotations.note: Affine spaces are formalized as `AddTorsor`s with `AffineEquiv` automorphisms, but transitivity of the affine group is not stated as such.

annotations.label: Projective linear group on projective space

anchor.snippet: The projective linear group PGL( n + 1, K ) and its subgroups

annotations.note: An anonymous `instance : MulAction G (ℙ K V)` provides the linear-group action on projectivization (projective space).

annotations.label: Isometries on images and patterns

anchor.snippet: The isometries of the plane act on the set of 2D images and patterns

annotations.note: The action of plane isometries on images/patterns is not formalized.

annotations.label: Category of G-sets

anchor.snippet: comprise the category of G -sets in which the objects are G -sets

annotations.note: `Action (Type u) G` is the category of G-sets (the docstring notes `V = Type` gives G-actions).

annotations.label: Galois group on a field

anchor.snippet: The Galois group of a field extension L / K acts on the field L

annotations.note: `AlgEquiv.applyMulSemiringAction` is the action of `L ≃ₐ[K] L` (the Galois group) on `L` by ring automorphisms.

annotations.label: Reals acting by time translation

anchor.snippet: acts on the phase space of

annotations.note: `Flow τ α` is a continuous additive-monoid action; time translation by ℝ on a phase space is the case `τ = ℝ`.

annotations.label: Reals acting on real functions

anchor.snippet: acts on the set of real functions of a real variable

annotations.note: The translation action of ℝ on real functions is not formalized as a specific instance.

annotations.label: Induced action on the power set

anchor.snippet: we can define an induced action of G on the power set

annotations.note: `Set.mulAction` is the pointwise action of `G` on `Set α`, i.e. the induced action on the power set.

annotations.label: Unit quaternions acting on R^3

anchor.snippet: The quaternions with norm 1 (the versors )

annotations.note: The action of unit quaternions on ℝ³ by rotation is not formalized as a group action.

annotations.label: Exponential object of G-sets

anchor.snippet: whose elements are G -equivariant maps

annotations.note: G-equivariant maps are `MulActionHom`, but the internal-hom/exponential object structure of the G-set category is not formalized as such.

annotations.label: Action groupoid

anchor.snippet: can be encoded by the action groupoid

annotations.note: `CategoryTheory.ActionCategory M X` is the action groupoid (groupoid when `M` is a group).

annotations.label: Morphism of G-sets (equivariant map)

anchor.snippet: a morphism from X to Y is a function

annotations.note: `MulActionHom` is the structure of an equivariant map between actions.

annotations.label: Isomorphism of G-sets

anchor.snippet: In this case f is called an isomorphism

annotations.note: Isomorphisms of G-sets are bijective `MulActionHom`s (or isos in the action category); there is no dedicated `MulActionEquiv` bundling.

annotations.label: Classification of regular/free/transitive actions

anchor.snippet: Every regular G action is isomorphic to the action of G on G

annotations.note: `orbitEquivQuotientStabilizer` realizes a transitive action as `G ⧸ stabilizer`, but the full classification up to G-set isomorphism is not stated as a single theorem.

annotations.label: G-sets form a Grothendieck topos

anchor.snippet: the collection of all G -sets forms a category

annotations.note: `Action (Type u) G` gives the category of G-sets, but it is not proven to be a Grothendieck topos.

annotations.label: Monoid actions

anchor.snippet: We can also consider actions of monoids on sets

annotations.note: `MulAction` is defined for any `Monoid`, so monoid actions are directly covered.

annotations.label: Actions on objects of a category

anchor.snippet: we can define actions of groups and monoids on objects of an arbitrary category

annotations.note: `Action V G` bundles a monoid action of `G` on an object of an arbitrary category `V` via `G →* End V`.

annotations.label: Group action as a functor

anchor.snippet: A (left) group action is then nothing but a (covariant) functor

annotations.note: `actionAsFunctor : SingleObj M ⥤ Type u` realizes an action as a functor from the one-object category.
